prevalence.trend: increasing due to air pollution and biomass fuel exposure

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: GOLD criteria adapted, clinical diagnosis in resource-limited settings

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: very limited spirometry access, <5% in rural areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: GOLD criteria, clinical diagnosis common (chronic cough/sputum), limited spirometry
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bronchodilators (salbutamol), theophylline, limited ICS access, clean cooking fuel initiatives, Ayurvedic options (e.g., Vasarishta)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Extreme air pollution (Delhi, Mumbai), biomass fuel use in 50% rural households, high TB coinfection, limited spirometry
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Ujjwala Yojana (clean cooking fuel program), affordable generic bronchodilators, Ayurvedic respiratory support
